JetBlue faces litigation over hazardous materials handling, highlighting carrier liability for in-flight safety incidents involving improperly managed dry ice.

Swissto12 to build small optical relay GEO satellite for Space Compass

Small satellite optical relay capabilities are expanding beyond traditional providers, creating new connectivity infrastructure opportunities for aviation networks.

Modern SMS frameworks shifted aviation from investigating accidents to preventing them through systematic risk identification and mitigation.

The iconic aircraft was once a workhorse of many fleets around the world.

Boeing's 757 retirement reflects market consolidation toward larger twin-aisle aircraft and changing airline economics post-9/11.

United Airlines To Debut New Polaris Suites Without Full Passenger Privacy

United's Polaris suite rollout faces certification delays, forcing compromise on promised privacy features and creating competitive vulnerability.

Industry Mar 23, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Optimizing airline, airport, and flight operations through advanced technologies is critical to managing growth while reducing aviation's environmental impact.

See MDPI →

Military Mar 22, 2026

The Tomcat’s Six Pack: The F-14 Six on Six Phoenix Test

Designed for fleet air defense, the F-14 was armed with a potent long-range missile and, with a powerful new radar, could engage multiple airborne targets at once. The United States Navy’s F-14 Tomcat was the most potent fleet defense aircraft of its time. Its AIM-54 Phoenix missile had a long ra...

The F-14 Tomcat's six-on-six Phoenix missile capability demonstrated revolutionary multi-target engagement for naval air defense.

See The Aviationist →

Military Mar 22, 2026

U.S. Army Receives DARPA’s Optionally Piloted H-60Mx Black Hawk

The U.S. Army will put the H-60Mx and Sikorsky’s MATRIX flight autonomy software through a rigorous operational testing campaign with real world logistics in contested scenarios. The U.S. Army received on Mar. 19, 2026, the H-60Mx Black Hawk Optionally Piloted Vehicle (OPV) at Fort Eustis, Virgin...

Army's optionally piloted H-60Mx represents autonomous rotorcraft advancement for contested logistics operations, signaling industry shift toward unmanned military aviation capabilities.

See The Aviationist →

SpaceX's orbital data center constellation represents a new commercial space application that could reshape satellite industry economics and aviation infrastructure dependencies.

How Quvia helps airlines throw out the IFC playbook and take control

The technology is evolving rapidly, including the antenna hardware. At least five or more broadband Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satell...

Airlines are gaining direct control over inflight connectivity through marketplace platforms like Quvia, enabling faster technology adoption and reduced vendor lock-in.

Video Shows Saab 340 Airborne Radar Flying in Ukraine

Ukraine appears to have received its first Airborne Early Warning and Control (AEW&C) aircraft, after the Swedish Saab 340 airborne radar wa...

Ukraine's acquisition of the Saab 340 AEW&C aircraft significantly enhances air defense capabilities against cruise missiles and drone threats.

The U.S. Air Force is progressing with the integrations tests of the AGM-181 Long Range Stand Off (LRSO) missile, the service...

F-22 Raptor stealth tank and pod development advances represent critical upgrades to maintain tactical superiority in contested airspace.
